explainability.findings: Reports outline mandates for explainability in AI models. Additionally, blog posts reference the requirement for AI systems to be explainable and aligned with human judgment.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party report, "Reshaping Software Engineering at Edward Jones with Generative AI," provides evidence for the **governance**, **explainability**, and **fairness** pillars of responsible AI. The report highlights a policy commitment to AI model governance, including validation, testing, and monitoring, as well as mandates for explainability and bias mitigation.
relevant_sources.title: Reshaping Software Engineering at Edward Jones with Generative AI

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post from Emerj provides evidence for **governance**, **transparency**, and **explainability**. It highlights Edward Jones' focus on developing AI-specific governance frameworks and guides for AI applications, demonstrating a commitment to responsible AI practices. The post also details the firm's use of generative AI for code analysis and mentions their reliance on both internal and vendor-provided AI tools, underscoring transparency in AI capabilities and usage. Furthermore, it touches upon the requirement for AI systems to be explainable and aligned with human judgment, supporting the explainability pillar.
relevant_sources.title: Emerj Analysis of AI Use Cases at Edward Jones

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: Reports outline mandates for bias mitigation in AI models. This indicates a commitment to addressing potential biases in AI systems.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party report, "Reshaping Software Engineering at Edward Jones with Generative AI," provides evidence for the **governance**, **explainability**, and **fairness** pillars of responsible AI. The report highlights a policy commitment to AI model governance, including validation, testing, and monitoring, as well as mandates for explainability and bias mitigation.
relevant_sources.title: Reshaping Software Engineering at Edward Jones with Generative AI

governance.findings: Edward Jones outlines its strategic approach to AI, including principles for data delivery and defining AI's role. Training documents detail programs for ethical and compliant generative AI use, while press releases mention requirements for AI to prove consistency, reliability, and safety before autonomous operation. Reports highlight a policy commitment to AI model governance, encompassing validation, testing, and monitoring, and blog posts describe a focus on developing AI-specific governance frameworks and guides.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by outlining Edward Jones's strategic approach to AI, including the principle of delivering data for specific reasons and defining the role of AI. Transparency is evidenced by the description of AI-generated answers for enterprise search and AI-driven "nudges" for advisors, which illustrate how AI enhances information access and assists client interactions.
relevant_sources.title: Edward Jones is All In on Its Real-time AI Strategy

relevant_sources.summary: This internal training document for Edward Jones's "Mack" generative AI tool provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars. It details a training program focused on applying operating principles for ethical and compliant use of genAI, indicating established governance practices. Furthermore, the document highlights transparency through the mention of a specific AI tool, the creation of a prompt library with measurable results, and the modeling of human skills alongside AI, all contributing to an understanding of how the tool is used and managed.
relevant_sources.title: Training APEX Awards Best Practice: Gen AI for You & I at Edward Jones

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for governance, oversight, and transparency in AI deployment. It details Edward Jones' pilot program for agentic AI in marketing, highlighting a mandatory human-in-the-loop process for refining AI-generated content, which demonstrates oversight. The company's explicit acknowledgment of AI's current limitations in delivering "nuance, emotional clarity, and a sense of humanity" supports transparency regarding AI capabilities, while the mention of requirements for AI to prove consistency, reliability, and safety before autonomous operation points to governance measures.
relevant_sources.title: Why Edward Jones' agentic AI trial comes with limits

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: Edward Jones describes how AI enhances information access and client interactions through AI-generated answers for enterprise search and AI-driven "nudges" for advisors. Training documents mention a specific generative AI tool, "Mack," detailing the creation of a prompt library and the modeling of human skills alongside AI. Edward Jones also explicitly acknowledges AI's current limitations and references its use of generative AI for code analysis and reliance on both internal and vendor-provided AI tools.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

summary.overall_findings: Based on 11 publicly available sources, Edward Jones' published materials address 5 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Press releases describe AI-generated answers for enterprise search and AI-driven "nudges" for advisors, while also detailing a mandatory human-in-the-loop process for refining AI-generated content in a pilot program for agentic AI in marketing. Fairness and explainability are addressed through documented policies, with reports outlining mandates for bias mitigation and the requirement for AI systems to be explainable and aligned with human judgment. Governance is also covered, with training documents detailing a program focused on applying operating principles for ethical and compliant use of generative AI. No qualifying public evidence was found for privacy or external accountability.
